title: Exquisite Garlicky Vietnamese Pork Bun Cha
description: A garlicky and fluffy Vietnamese dish featuring red wine vinegar, served in a light and refreshing bun cha style, egg-free and friendly for all diets.
instructions: Heat the vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the sliced pork shoulder and cook until browned, about 5 minutes.
Add the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute.
In a small bowl, whisk together the red wine vinegar, fish sauce, brown sugar, and black pepper.
Pour the vinegar mixture over the pork and garlic, and stir to combine.
Cook for an additional 2 minutes, until the sauce has thickened.
Cook the rice noodles according to the package instructions.
Assemble the bun cha by placing the cooked noodles in a bowl, topping with the pork and garlic mixture, and garnishing with chopped green onions and mint leaves.
Use nigiri-shaping technique to shape the pork mixture into small balls before serving.
Garnish with additional mint leaves and green onions, if desired.
